description: TabMail Message Header Chip experiment API — paints the iOS-style action chip into the message preview header (#expandedButtonsBox) and (Phase 2) emits an event when the chip is clicked. Passive painter: never writes action state.

functions.name: init
functions.description: Register window listeners and paint chips into all currently-displayed surfaces. Idempotent.

functions.name: shutdown
functions.description: Detach all delegations/observers, remove all chips, unregister window listener.

functions.name: refreshAll
functions.description: Repaint the chip on every preview pane / message-display tab / standalone message window. Idempotent. Triggered from MV3 by messageDisplay.onMessagesDisplayed and by actionCache write/clear paths. The experiment also self-triggers on header DOM re-renders via an internal MutationObserver (no MV3 call needed for that).

events.name: onActionChipClick
events.description: Fired when the user clicks/activates the header chip. The payload identifies the message whose chip was clicked — MV3 should act on that exact message regardless of the current 3-pane selection (which can drift between paint and click, and is empty in standalone message windows).

weMsgId.description: WebExtension message id of the chip's message
